title: Cannot allocate memory for etcd
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I'm unable to run an `etcd` container in a podman VM. It panics with the error message "cannot allocate memory", which I expect is related to podman configuration, although I can't figure out what the issue might be. I'm on a Mac M1 in case that might be related.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman with `brew install podman`

2. Init the podman VM with `podman machine init --cpus 4 -m 8192 --rootful --now`

3. Run the ETCD container with `podman run gcr.io/etcd-development/etcd:v3.4.15`

**Describe the results you received:**

```none
[WARNING] Deprecated '--logger=capnslog' flag is set; use '--logger=zap' flag instead

2022-07-30 06:02:18.681067 W | etcdmain: no data-dir provided, using default data-dir ./default.etcd
[WARNING] Deprecated '--logger=capnslog' flag is set; use '--logger=zap' flag instead

go.etcd.io/etcd/mvcc/backend.newBackend(0xc000274360, 0x1b, 0x5f5e100, 0x2710, 0x109eb1b, 0x5, 0x280000000, 0x0, 0x0, 0xc00003bf10)

	/tmp/etcd-release-3.4.15/etcd/release/etcd/etcdserver/backend.go:74 +0x78
created by go.etcd.io/etcd/etcdserver.openBackend
	/tmp/etcd-release-3.4.15/etcd/release/etcd/etcdserver/backend.go:73 +0x109
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

If I run the container on Docker Desktop for Mac, the container successfully runs:

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I've tried reinstalling podman, and also running `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.1.1/bin/podman-mac-helper install`, but neither resolved the issue. Running `ulimit -v` inside the VM as per [this issue](https://github-com.translate.goog/apache/servicecomb-service-center/issues/383?_x_tr_sl=zh-CN&_x_tr_tl=en&_x_tr_hl=en&_x_tr_pto=wapp) shows

```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical, Mac M1, 16 GB memory

comments.body:
This looks like the container is running out of memory within the VM. Does etcd require a certain amount of memory to run?

@vrothberg @giuseppe thoughts?
The same error happens if I `podman machine set -m 10240 && podman machine start`. Also, the same error happens with the latest version of the etcd image (`gcr.io/etcd-development/etcd:latest`)
Any chance you can try the container with `--privileged`? I wonder if it's trying some sort of memory-manipulation syscall that's blocked by Seccomp and/or capabilities

Unfortunately it still returns the same error:
```
~ $ podman run --privileged gcr.io/etcd-development/etcd:latest
2022-07-31 18:32:15.830618 I | etcdmain: etcd Version: 3.3.12
2022-07-31 18:32:15.833762 I | etcdmain: Git SHA: d57e8b8
2022-07-31 18:32:15.833817 I | etcdmain: Go Version: go1.10.8
2022-07-31 18:32:15.833859 I | etcdmain: Go OS/Arch: linux/amd64
2022-07-31 18:32:15.833919 I | etcdmain: setting maximum number of CPUs to 4, total number of available CPUs is 4
2022-07-31 18:32:15.834553 W | etcdmain: no data-dir provided, using default data-dir ./default.etcd
2022-07-31 18:32:15.847864 I | embed: listening for peers on http://localhost:2380
2022-07-31 18:32:15.849203 I | embed: listening for client requests on localhost:2379
2022-07-31 18:32:15.858635 C | mvcc/backend: cannot open database at default.etcd/member/snap/db (cannot allocate memory)
panic: cannot open database at default.etcd/member/snap/db (cannot allocate memory)
```
what is the ulimit for virtual memory (`ulimit -v`)?

Here's a `docker` arm64 etcd "work-around" for Apple Silicon. Hopefully this can be transposed easily to podman:

```bash
export DOCKER_DEFAULT_PLATFORM=linux/arm64/v8
docker run --env ETCD_UNSUPPORTED_ARCH=arm64 gcr.io/etcd-development/etcd:v3.4.20-arm64
```
... and the `docker compose` equivalent (with $PROJECT and volume mount):

```bash
export PROJECT=my-project
mkdir -p ~/data/${PROJECT}/etcd
docker compose -p $PROJECT up
```

```yaml
# docker-compose.yaml
version: '3.1'

services:
  etcd:
    image: gcr.io/etcd-development/etcd:v3.4.20-arm64
    hostname: etcd.${PROJECT}
    container_name: etcd.${PROJECT}
    platform: linux/arm64/v8
    volumes:
      - ~/data/${PROJECT}/etcd:/default.etcd:rw
    environment:
      - ALLOW_NONE_AUTHENTICATION=yes
      - ETCD_UNSUPPORTED_ARCH=arm64

networks:
  default:
    name: ${PROJECT}   
```

thanks for the recipe!

I am closing this issue since there is nothing Podman can do about it

yeah, this looks like a compatibility issue with etcd amd64 docker and Apple silicon (and def not specific to podman) -- but happily in looking for a fix I came across this post and learned about podman ... looks cool! :)

Thank you! Just tested the following it it worked perfectly on my M1 Pro:

```
podman run -it --rm --env ETCD_UNSUPPORTED_ARCH=arm64 gcr.io/etcd-development/etcd:v3.4.20-arm64
```
